"  
Sec. 1  
[Consider the equation involving a fourth power of x and an absolute value term. The equation is symmetric about the y-axis because of the absolute value. This symmetry implies that the graph of the equation will have points that mirror each other across the y-axis. Therefore, the number of solutions will be even, unless there is a solution at x = 0.]  

Sec. 2  
[To determine the number of real solutions, analyze the behavior of the function on the positive side of the x-axis. Since the absolute value of x is equal to x when x is non-negative, the equation simplifies to a polynomial equation on this interval. By evaluating the function at key points and considering its monotonicity, it can be determined that there are two real solutions on the positive side. Due to the symmetry of the equation, there will also be two real solutions on the negative side. Thus, the total number of real solutions is four.]  
"
